description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped stone with a roughly triangular profile and a slightly tapered end. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of smooth and jagged areas, with visible chipping and natural indentations. The coloration is predominantly a light grayish-beige, interspersed with subtle brown and tan patches, suggesting mineral inclusions or weathering. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and mass indicate it could serve as a substantial obstacle or boundary marker in an urban environment. The overall appearance suggests it has been naturally weathered, with some smoother facets likely caused by prolonged exposure to the elements.
description: A large, irregularly shaped light gray stone with a rough, weathered surface and subtle brownish patches. Its solid, dense form and uneven texture make it a prominent natural obstacle.
description_view_0: A narrow, pointed end of the stone, showing its tapered profile and rough, angular surface.
description_view_1: The broadest side of the stone, displaying a large, flat surface with visible chipping and subtle color variations.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow end, highlighting the stone's thickness and irregular, jagged edges.
description_view_3: Another wide side, similar to view 1 but with more visible surface texture and a slight curve along the base.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and mineral veining with irregular, natural chipping; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with some smooth facets and chipped edges, indicating exposure to rain and foot traffic.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones, providing a visual transition between natural and built elements.
